notes: Start small; this graph can be expanded. The point is to give an agent the central dependency structure immediately.

claims.name: Stability Assumption
claims.claim: The boundary between what an optimization system is pursuing and what it must model to act effectively can remain coherent — not perfect, but coherently specifiable — as modeling depth increases in coupled environments.
claims.framework_position: target assumption under investigation; argued to fail under O_OWT conditions

claims.name: Proxy-Convergence Lemma (PCL)
claims.claim: Fixed finite specifications decouple from the substrate or state they were intended to track under sustained optimization pressure in O_OWT environments.
claims.status: Stage 4 argument; structural pressure on fixed-specification strategy class

claims.name: Adaptive Gradient Complexity (AGC)
claims.claim: Bounded dynamic tracking fails as the optimizer's own interventions generate new causal structure faster than any bounded tracking process can absorb.
claims.status: Stage 4 argument; empirical hinge is DBST-M1

claims.name: Informational-Causal Incompatibility (ICI)
claims.claim: Variables required for prediction accuracy cannot remain excluded from action-governing policy without reproducing the boundary problem at the prediction layer.

claims.name: OP4d Exhaustiveness Obligation
claims.claim: PCL, AGC, and ICI are exhaustive over all identified finite non-intrinsic objective-boundary strategies under O_OWT conditions.
claims.status: Stage 4 candidate architecture; exhaustiveness unproven; central open problem
claims.falsified_by: A fourth strategy class satisfying all three stability conditions simultaneously

claims.name: Specification-Coherence Bottleneck
claims.claim: Under O_OWT conditions, no known finite separable objective-boundary strategy remains stably adequate under accurate coupled modeling.
claims.status: Stage 4; follows from OP4d IF OP4d is exhaustive
claims.conditional: Holds if and only if OP4d exhaustiveness is confirmed

claims.name: O_OWT Conditions
claims.claim: Open, shared, adaptive, non-resettable environments where optimization at scale changes the conditions that make optimization viable.

claims.name: DBST-M1
claims.claim: DBST-M1 can test whether endogenous novelty generation outpaces bounded tracking capacity.
claims.status: proposed empirical test; DBST-M0 did not isolate causal propagation
